subject: Sonar cleanup for integrity-audit

Patch Set 2:

Not sure about the removal of the "isDebugEnabled" Drew.   They have an impact on performance, provided that otherwise string concatenation will be done at logging method invokation.   It can be mitigated and it will be okthough with log.debug("xx {} {}",a,b) since that won't do the concatenated until the level is checked by the logger, and not sure if FlexLogger supports that. Here perhaps is tolerable because I don't think this code will execute that often, don't think it is a good thing to change the isDebugEnabled, unless the {} mechanism is used.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Looks ok to me now that you have fixed the tempBadParams issue.

Patch Set 2:

Kevin, Jorge's question is regarding the removal of the isDebugEnabled statements. Please let us know If that was done to satisfy Sonar generating critical issues. If not, then the performance is degraded by eliminating them.

Patch Set 2:

That's what I suspected. I'd like Jorge to weigh in. Are all the debug messages necessary? If so, then the addition of the class is a good idea. thanks!

Patch Set 2:

I recommend to put the "isDebugEnabled" back, or alternate solution with String format that I don't think is supported by FlexLogger, easiest to put isDebugEnabled back.   String concatenation is a well known performance killer for blocks of code that executes frequently, and the concatenation that I see here is serious of many pieces of data.   We want to avoid this before the checking of the log level, so we don't want to concatenate if the level is not DEBUG which will be >99.9% of the times.  

Based on what Drew is saying, the underlying problem seems to be that sonar complains if the number of nested blocks is >N, and level N seems to be hit with the isDebug check.     Another solution, would be to create methods instead only for the debug, but go a few levels up and see if it a larger block of code can be pulled out.  This solution will make more sense if it makes the code easier to read.  Note that within eclipse and any other IDE, there are some refactor functions that could extract the method from you automatically.    

Other option is to just not deal with this issue, being a major, is not required work for this sprint to dealt with, we could then assign to Kevin as domain expert in this area, for any refactoring if needed indeed.
